questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'The Congress System: A Theoretical Analysis'?
questions.explanation: Karl W. Deutsch is the author of the book 'The Congress System: A Theoretical Analysis'.

questions.q: What is the main argument of Giovanni Sartori in his book 'The Theory of Democracy Revisited'?
opts: (A) The Congress System is a stable system
(B) The Congress System is a fragile system
(C) The Congress System is a system of fragmented politics
(D) The Congress System is a system of authoritarian politics
questions.explanation: Giovanni Sartori argues that the Congress System is a system of fragmented politics where multiple parties and interest groups lead to instability.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a stable Congress System?
opts: (C) Fragmentation of parties and interest groups
questions.explanation: A stable Congress System requires the presence of a strong opposition to provide a check on the ruling party.
questions.topic: Characteristics of a Stable Congress System

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the situation where a single party dominates the Congress System?
questions.explanation: A dominant party system is a situation where a single party dominates the Congress System.
questions.topic: Characteristics of a Stable Congress System

questions.q: Which of the following is a challenge to the Congress System?
opts: (C) Fragmentation of parties and interest groups
(D) Presence of a strong civil society
questions.explanation: Fragmentation of parties and interest groups can lead to instability and challenges to the Congress System.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the situation where multiple parties and interest groups have equal influence in the Congress System?
questions.explanation: A multipolar system is a situation where multiple parties and interest groups have equal influence in the Congress System.
questions.topic: Characteristics of a Stable Congress System

questions.q: Which of the following is a consequence of the Congress System?
questions.explanation: The Congress System can lead to increased political stability due to the presence of a strong opposition.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the situation where the Congress System is unable to make decisions due to the presence of multiple parties and interest groups?
questions.explanation: Gridlock is a situation where the Congress System is unable to make decisions due to the presence of multiple parties and interest groups.

questions.q: Which of the following is a challenge to the Congress System in the Indian context?
opts: (B) Dominance of the Indian National Congress
(C) Presence of a strong civil society
questions.explanation: The rise of regional parties is a challenge to the Congress System in the Indian context.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the situation where the Congress System is unable to respond to changing societal needs?
questions.explanation: Inertia is a situation where the Congress System is unable to respond to changing societal needs.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a fragile Congress System?
opts: (C) Fragmentation of parties and interest groups
questions.explanation: A fragile Congress System is characterized by the fragmentation of parties and interest groups.
questions.topic: Characteristics of a Fragile Congress System

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the situation where the Congress System is unable to provide effective governance?
questions.explanation: A governance crisis is a situation where the Congress System is unable to provide effective governance.

questions.q: Which of the following is a consequence of the Congress System in the Indian context?
questions.explanation: The Congress System in the Indian context has led to increased political stability.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the situation where the Congress System is unable to respond to external pressures?
questions.explanation: Insecurity is a situation where the Congress System is unable to respond to external pressures.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a stable Congress System in the Indian context?
opts: (B) Dominance of the Indian National Congress
(C) Fragmentation of parties and interest groups
questions.explanation: A stable Congress System in the Indian context requires the presence of a strong opposition.
questions.topic: Characteristics of a Stable Congress System

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the situation where the Congress System is unable to provide effective representation to marginalized groups?
questions.explanation: A representation crisis is a situation where the Congress System is unable to provide effective representation to marginalized groups.

questions.q: Which of the following is a challenge to the Congress System in the context of globalization?
opts: (B) Dominance of the Indian National Congress
(C) Presence of a strong civil society
questions.explanation: Increased globalization is a challenge to the Congress System in the context of globalization.

questions.q: What is the term used to describe the situation where the Congress System is unable to respond to changing societal values?
questions.explanation: A cultural crisis is a situation where the Congress System is unable to respond to changing societal values.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a fragile Congress System in the Indian context?
opts: (B) Dominance of the Indian National Congress
(C) Fragmentation of parties and interest groups
questions.explanation: A fragile Congress System in the Indian context is characterized by the fragmentation of parties and interest groups.
questions.topic: Characteristics of a Fragile Congress System
